In baseball terminology, "ball four" refers to a situation where a batter receives four pitches that are called balls, resulting in a walk (also known as a base on balls). When a batter "takes" a ball, it means they did not swing at the pitch and the umpire called it a ball. So, in baseball terms, when a batter is at the plate, the pitcher throws pitches.
